findings.message: Added `requests` is unpinned (no version constraint), unlike every other dependency in this manifest which uses a `>=` floor. Unpinned deps make resolves non-reproducible and let a future major break the build silently. Also: `httpx>=0.28` is already in the dev group and `fastapi`'s stack ships `httpx`/`anyio`-based clients — for new runtime HTTP needs, prefer the async `httpx` already used in this project rather than introducing the synchronous `requests` library as a second HTTP client.
findings.suggestion: Either drop `requests` and use `httpx` (already present, async-native, integrates with the FastAPI/OTel stack) or, if `requests` is truly needed, pin a floor: `"requests>=2.32"`.

findings.message: `requests` is in maintenance mode (low feature velocity) and is synchronous-only, which can block the FastAPI event loop if called from request handlers. The project already standardizes on async (FastAPI + OTel async instrumentation); adding a sync HTTP client risks subtle latency/throughput regressions and a second OTel instrumentation surface to wire up.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on `httpx` for outbound HTTP (sync or async API available, supported by `opentelemetry-instrumentation-httpx`).
